hypothesis: If a small language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-focused literature and large chemical structure corpora, then after supervised fine-tuning on QMOF and hMOF using MOFid text inputs it will achieve lower MAE than structure-based baselines (e.g., CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer), especially with limited (1–10k) labeled data, because domain-specific pre-training transfers chemical knowledge that compensates for the absence of 3D structural information.
